steps.title: Identify the key constraint on the introduction
steps.content: Since the audience is already familiar with MBC, an effective introduction can't just list Walker as one of many. It needs to establish his significance by connecting him to MBC's core mission: inspiring music students with accomplished Black classical composers.

steps.title: Apply this constraint to the choices
steps.content:
For an audience already familiar with the MBC project, the introduction should name Walker as a notable figure within MBC without re-explaining the project itself.

Choice C (“Among the many talented figures included in the MBC project is classical music composer George Theophilus Walker”) does exactly that — it situates Walker within MBC and sets up a discussion of his career.

Choice A diminishes him as one of many, choice B gives only biographical dates, and choice D re-explains MBC’s mission to an audience that already knows it.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
